Title: Emergence of isochorismate-based salicylic acid biosynthesis within Brassicales
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immunity, abiotic stress tolerance, and growth regulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: pathogen-inducible in Arabidopsis IC pathway context; exact tissue pattern NA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes SA and SAG accumulation when coexpressed with ICS1 and PBS3
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium transient expression in leaves; pathogen-inducible SA context in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana pathway reconstitution; Arabidopsis eds5 complementation; confocal localization; immunoblotting; site-directed mutagenesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS5 from Brassicales species that diversified after the divergence of Carica papaya supports SA biosynthesis. 2. EDS5 underwent neofunctionalization after duplication of ancestral EDS5A. 3. Chloroplast membrane localization alone is insufficient; specific C-lobe residues are required for SA-biosynthetic function.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/de novo transcriptome assembly for additional Brassicales species; NCBI SRA PRJNA1201569
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for reconstructing Brassicales-type IC-based SA biosynthesis; likely must be stacked with functional ICS and PBS3
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although hypothesized to transport isochorismate from chloroplast to cytosol, direct transport evidence was not shown in this paper.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immunity, abiotic stress tolerance, and growth regulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: does not support SA and SAG accumulation in the IC pathway reconstitution assay
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana pathway reconstitution; confocal localization; immunoblotting
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS5H is the duplicated sister lineage of EDS5 in Brassicales. 2. Unlike EDS5, EDS5H failed to support SA biosynthesis in reconstitution assays. 3. Data support retention of ancestral/non-IC-pathway function rather than neofunctionalization for SA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not suitable as a substitute for EDS5 in engineering IC-based SA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested as a primary comparison for EDS5 neofunctionalization.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: multiple non-Brassicales species and Carica papaya
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Glycine max; Zea mays; Oryza sativa
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immunity, abiotic stress tolerance, and growth regulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not pathogen-inducible in tested non-Brassicales species
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; endogenous in soybean, maize, rice
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: nonfunctional for IC-based SA biosynthesis in reconstitution and mutant analyses indicate dispensable for SA accumulation in soybean, maize, and rice
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pathogen infection in soybean; basal conditions in maize and rice; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana pathway reconstitution; CRISPR mutants in soybean and rice; EMS mutant in maize; SA quantification; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ancestral EDS5A homologs from non-Brassicales species and Carica papaya failed to support SA biosynthesis in reconstitution assays. 2. Mutations of EDS5A homologs in soybean, maize, and rice did not reduce SA accumulation. 3. These findings support that EDS5 neofunctionalization for IC-based SA biosynthesis is restricted to a Brassicales clade.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/de novo transcriptome assembly for Reseda odorata, Batis maritima, and Limnanthes douglasii; NCBI SRA PRJNA1201569
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In tomato, homozygous Sleds5A mutants could not be obtained, suggesting a possible essential developmental role for EDS5A there
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Ancestral EDS5A is insufficient for engineering the IC pathway; replacement with neofunctionalized Brassicales EDS5 is required
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents the ancestral homolog class rather than a single locus; grouped because the paper’s conclusion concerns functional class-level divergence.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: isomerizes chorismate to isochorismate in chloroplasts
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; enhanced activity decisive for Brassicales IC pathway evolution
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immunity, abiotic stress tolerance, and growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: pathogen inducible in Arabidopsis; ICS1 induced upon pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; pathogen-induced in Arabidopsis complementation context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana pathway reconstitution; Arabidopsis sid2/ics1 complementation; site-directed mutagenesis; confocal microscopy; immunoblotting; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: functional Brassicales ICS1 promotes strong SA and SAG accumulation; AtICS1 catalytic and salt-bridge mutants reduce or abolish this effect
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 activity for SA biosynthesis is strongly enhanced in Brassicales species after divergence of Carica papaya. 2. Functional ICS1 genes from Brassicaceae, Tarenaya hassleriana, and Capparis spinosa restore SA biosynthesis in Arabidopsis ics1 and in N. benthamiana reconstitution. 3. Residues E366, R135, D346, and R367 are critical for activity, supporting a salt-bridge network model for enhanced catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream enzyme for reconstructing high-efficiency IC-based SA biosynthesis, but requires compatible EDS5 and PBS3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: C. papaya ICS is chloroplast-localized but nonfunctional in the assay, indicating localization alone is insufficient.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immunity, abiotic stress tolerance, and growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not pathogen inducible in Arabidopsis according to cited/mentioned evidence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Arabidopsis and other Brassicaceae ICS2 homolog activity increased SA accumulation in reconstitution assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS2-clade proteins from Brassicaceae also showed strong SA-biosynthetic activity in the reconstitution assay. 2. However, the paper emphasizes enhanced ICS activity as a Brassicales trait rather than ICS2-specific specialization. 3. Arabidopsis ICS2 is not pathogen-inducible unlike ICS1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May serve as an alternative enzymatic module in engineering, though native inducibility differs from ICS1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was directly assayed in Results, but ICS1 is the principal reference gene.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase / isochorismate-glutamate ligase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: conjugates glutamate to isochorismate to form isochorismate-9-glutamate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key specialized step; functional clade restricted to Brassicales lineage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immunity, abiotic stress tolerance, and growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana pathway reconstitution; phylogenetic analysis; localization; immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: functional PBS3 from Brassicales species that diversified after Carica papaya promotes SA and SAG accumulation when combined with ICS1 and EDS5
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBS3 function is restricted to a Brassicales clade that diversified after the divergence of Carica papaya and Moringa oleifera lineages. 2. Brassicaceae PBS3 homologs and some Tarenaya hassleriana/Capparis spinosa Subclade A1a homologs support SA biosynthesis. 3. Closest non-Clade A homologs from non-Brassicales species and C. papaya are nonfunctional in the assay.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential cytosolic module to combine with ICS and EDS5 for synthetic reconstruction of the Brassicales IC pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only closely related Subclade A1a GH3 proteins retain PBS3-like SA-biosynthetic activity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: conjugates amino acid to isochorismate with weaker activity than PBS3
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: IC-amino acid conjugate leading to SA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms SA-producing intermediate with weaker efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immunity, abiotic stress tolerance, and growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N. benthamiana pathway reconstitution; localization; immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports SA biosynthesis weakly/redundantly relative to PBS3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.7 is one of the only Arabidopsis GH3 proteins besides PBS3 that shows SA-biosynthetic activity in the reconstitution assay. 2. It belongs to Subclade A1a together with PBS3. 3. Its activity is weaker and redundant relative to PBS3.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible backup or auxiliary gene, but weaker than PBS3 for pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was functionally assayed in Results as a closely related active GH3.
